request.context_summary: Security review of Terraform modules for serverless blog infrastructure (CDK to Terraform migration)

findings.issue: Cognito app client enables the implicit OAuth flow, which exposes tokens in URLs and bypasses PKCE protections.
findings.suggestion: Remove the implicit flow and use authorization code flow with PKCE only (e.g., keep only "code").

findings.issue: Callback URL is hard-coded to a placeholder domain; if left unchanged, auth codes/tokens can be redirected to an unintended host.
findings.suggestion: Make callback URLs environment-specific (variable input) and restrict them to the actual admin/public domains.

findings.issue: The app client does not enable "prevent_user_existence_errors", which allows user enumeration via auth error responses.
findings.suggestion: Set `prevent_user_existence_errors = "ENABLED"` on the user pool client to reduce enumeration risk.

findings.issue: All Posts-domain Lambdas share a single role with full DynamoDB write/delete privileges, including public endpoints (get_public_post/list_posts). This violates least privilege and increases blast radius if a public function is abused.
findings.suggestion: Split roles by function sensitivity (read-only role for public endpoints, read/write role for admin endpoints) and attach narrower policies.

findings.issue: Categories functions use a shared role with write permissions even for the public list endpoint, allowing unintended writes if that public path is exploited.
findings.suggestion: Create a separate read-only role/policy for `list_categories` and keep write permissions only on authenticated/admin functions.

findings.issue: Image bucket CORS allows any origin and any header, which broadens where pre-signed uploads can be used if a URL leaks.
findings.suggestion: Restrict CORS to known admin origins and only the headers/methods required for uploads.

findings.issue: CloudFront OAC bucket policies rely only on SourceArn; without SourceAccount, they are less protected against confused-deputy scenarios.

response.summary: Reviewed Terraform modules with a security focus; found OAuth client configuration risks, least-privilege gaps for public Lambdas, and a few S3 hardening items.
